risk_rationale: The new Australian Children's Online Privacy Code introduces stricter privacy protections for children, which may require immediate changes to TikTok's minor protection standards to ensure compliance.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Account privacy for 13-15 years: Private by default, no opt-out allowed
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Strengthened privacy protections for children online
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The new code may require additional privacy measures beyond the current default settings for minors aged 13-15.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Account privacy for 16-17 years: Public optional, EU requires explicit consent
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Strengthened privacy protections for children online
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The code may necessitate stricter privacy controls or consent mechanisms for minors aged 16-17.

remediations.title: Enhance Privacy Controls for Minors
remediations.description: Implement additional privacy measures for accounts of users under 18 in Australia, ensuring compliance with the new Children's Online Privacy Code.
acceptance_criteria: All accounts for users under 18 in Australia have enhanced privacy settings
Consent mechanisms are updated to align with the new code

business_impact: Failure to comply with the new privacy code could result in regulatory penalties and damage to brand reputation in Australia.
recommended_actions: Conduct a detailed legal review of the Children's Online Privacy Code
Develop a project plan to implement necessary changes to privacy settings
Engage with Australian regulators to ensure alignment and compliance
